payload.text:
Create a modern, professional, mobile-responsive AC Services website for an Indian service business that provides all types of air conditioner services including AC Installation, Uninstallation, AMC (Annual Maintenance Contract), Repairs, Gas Refilling, Cleaning & Maintenance.

The website should be designed for high conversion, easy booking, and trust-building, suitable for local service customers.

🎯 WEBSITE GOAL

Generate service bookings

Sell AMC subscription plans

Enable online payments

Build customer trust

Rank well on Google (SEO-friendly)

🧱 PAGES & SECTIONS REQUIRED
1️⃣ HOME PAGE

Design a clean, professional home page with:

Hero Section

Headline:
“Professional AC Services at Your Doorstep”

Subheading:
Installation | Repair | AMC Plans | All AC Brands

CTA Buttons:
✅ Book Service Now
✅ View AMC Plans

Service Highlights

AC Installation

AC Repair

AC Gas Refill

AC Cleaning

AMC Plans

Emergency Support

Why Choose Us

Trained & Verified Technicians

Same-Day Service

Affordable Pricing

Genuine Spare Parts

Service Warranty

Brands We Service

Voltas

LG

Daikin

Samsung

Hitachi

Blue Star

Panasonic

All AC Brands

Customer Testimonials

Star ratings

Real customer feedback cards

Service Areas

List cities / areas (editable from admin)

2️⃣ SERVICES PAGE

Create individual service cards with detail pages for:

✔ AC Installation

Split AC Installation

Window AC Installation

New & Old AC

Wall drilling, fitting, testing

✔ AC Repair

No cooling

Water leakage

Noise issue

PCB repair

Compressor issue

✔ AC Gas Refilling

R22, R32, R410A

Leak check included

✔ AC Cleaning & Servicing

Wet servicing

Foam cleaning

Jet wash

Anti-bacterial treatment

✔ AC Uninstallation & Relocation

Each service should include:

Description

Price starting from

“Book Now” button

3️⃣ AMC PLANS PAGE (VERY IMPORTANT)

Create subscription-based AMC plans:

AMC Plan Cards

🟢 Basic AMC

2 Services / Year

₹XXXX / Year

🔵 Standard AMC

3 Services / Year

Priority Support

₹XXXX / Year

🟣 Premium AMC

4 Services / Year

Free Inspection

Discount on Spares

₹XXXX / Year

Features:

Online purchase

Auto-renew option

Service reminders

Customer login to track AMC

4️⃣ BOOK SERVICE PAGE

Create a simple booking form with:

Name

Mobile Number

Email

Address

Select Service

Select Date & Time

Select AC Type (Split/Window)

Notes

Submit Button

5️⃣ PAYMENT INTEGRATION

Integrate Indian payment gateways:

Razorpay / Stripe

UPI

Debit / Credit Card

Net Banking

Wallets

Allow payments for:

One-time services

AMC plans

Advance booking

6️⃣ CUSTOMER DASHBOARD

Login / Signup

View bookings

View AMC plan details

Service history

Download invoices

Manage profile

7️⃣ ADMIN PANEL

Admin should be able to:

Manage services & pricing

Create/edit AMC plans

View bookings

Assign technicians

Manage payments

Manage customers

Update service areas

Send SMS / WhatsApp notifications

8️⃣ CONTACT PAGE

Contact form

Phone number

WhatsApp chat button

Google Map location

Business hours

9️⃣ SEO & TECH REQUIREMENTS

SEO-friendly URLs

Meta titles & descriptions

Fast loading

Mobile responsive

WhatsApp floating button

Call Now button

SSL security

Google Analytics ready

🎨 DESIGN STYLE

Professional & clean UI

Blue & white color theme (cooling feel)

Icons for services

Modern fonts

Trust badges

⚙️ TECH STACK (SUGGESTION)

Frontend: React / Next.js

Backend: Node.js / Firebase

Database: MongoDB / Supabase

Hosting: Vercel / Netlify

Payment: Razorpay

📌 EXTRA FEATURES (OPTIONAL)

Technician tracking

Coupon codes

Referral system

WhatsApp booking
Use vibrant colors, clean UI, smooth animations, and make the website fully
responsive for mobile and desktop. Create a professional logo and ensure all pages and links are fully interactive. Include a
footer with address, phone number, email, and social media links.

**Analyzing the Brief**

I've been meticulously examining the user's detailed request for an AC services website. The mobile-first design and React/Tailwind CSS framework are clearly priorities. I'm focusing on dissecting each page and section to ensure a robust, feature-rich, and user-friendly outcome. The goal is to translate this comprehensive brief into a concrete development plan.


**Defining the Structure**

The project structure is taking shape. I'm leaning heavily towards a `HashRouter` for navigation, given the environment limitations. All the pages are now mapped out: Home, Services, AMC, Book Service, Customer Dashboard (simulated), Admin Panel (simulated), and Contact. I've begun outlining the core functionality, including booking forms, mock payments for AMC, simulated login/signup, and rudimentary admin data management, all adhering to the chosen blue and white aesthetic.


**Mapping the Components**

I've finalized the component breakdown, with separate folders for Layout, UI, Forms, and Pages.  Key types, data constants, and a simulated context for user and booking data are also in place.  I'm now focusing on the core implementation. This includes state management with Context API for `currentUser` and booking data, with icons from Lucide, and placeholder images from picsum.photos.


**Building the Framework**

I'm now deep in the code, making quick progress. `HashRouter` is integrated.  I've put in `lucide-react` icons and Tailwind prefixes like `md:` and `lg:` for responsiveness. To keep the project manageable, I've decided to condense some of the smaller UI components. I'm focusing on the app's fundamental building blocks, from setting up the index files and types to generating constants. The navigation bar is also now responsive. I'm focusing on "CoolBreeze AC Services" as the title.


**Defining the Pages**

I've now mapped out all the individual pages. I'm focusing on the component-level details. The footer, the home page's key sections, the services list, AMC pricing, the booking form, the user dashboard, the admin view, and the contact page are all defined. I've also added the login page. Now, I'm working to connect all of these components into the main app structure.


**Confirming Scope Adjustments**

I've re-examined the project scope.  Focusing on a standard web app, I'll streamline the development by mocking payment and sticking to the stated aesthetic. I'm finalizing the file structure, aiming for a well-organized codebase with 18 key files, including React components, context, and constants. This structured approach will enable efficient development. I'll utilize `lucide-react` for icons and incorporate a large image hero section with an overlay.


**Incorporating the Details**

I've got the design down to the specifics now. I'm focusing on the component styles, using Tailwind's shadow and rounded features to build cards. Colors are set, with blue, sky, and orange leading the way. Brand placeholders will be used in place of SVGs and I'll include a simple list for service areas. I am planning on adding a status update in the dashboard for technician assignment. Let's start building!

**Troubleshooting Lucide-React**

I'm currently investigating a reported "Uncaught SyntaxError" with lucide-react, specifically regarding an undefined export. The goal is to identify and resolve this issue, potentially with revised import statements or dependency adjustments. I'm focusing on the exact nature of the missing export.


**Analyzing Icon Imports**

I've discovered the `Tool` icon in `lucide-react` might be the root of the "Uncaught SyntaxError." Examining `pages/Home.tsx`, I found the likely culprit is the `Tool` icon, which doesn't seem to be a direct export in newer versions. Considering the context of "Expert Technicians," I'll replace it with `Wrench`. I'm now verifying there are no other instances of `Tool` to address.


**Reviewing Code References**

I've scanned all the code and confirmed that `pages/Home.tsx` is the *only* file using the problematic `Tool` icon. No other instances were found across the project. Replacing `Tool` with `Wrench` seems to be the comprehensive fix, which I've now implemented. The issue appears fully resolved now that the `Tool` import is removed in favor of `Wrench`.


**Fixing Icon Errors**

I'm now implementing the replacement of the `Tool` icon with `Wrench` in `Home.tsx`. The import statement has been adjusted to include `Wrench`, and the component usage updated accordingly. **Implementing Styling Updates**

I'm now focusing on integrating the `text-brand-700` styling to selected details in the `Booking.tsx` page, specifically the values displayed in the service summary. I also need to make sure the error handling is working correctly.

**Enhancing Home Page Display**

I'm now completely redesigning the `Home.tsx` component, replacing the placeholder AMC banner with dynamic plan cards. I'm focusing on the gradient styling; I'm using `from-orange-400 to-yellow-500` for the recommended plan. I've designed the visual layout within the `AMCSeciton` component. **Reviewing Select Initialization**

I'm now revisiting the logic around the `select` element in `Booking.tsx` to understand the implications of initializing `formData.selectedId`. Now, I will calculate the default `selectedId` using the default service type to prevent an empty value and ensure the correct item is selected on page load and that if neither preSelectedPlanId nor preSelectedServiceId is set, one-time service is preselected and default to the first service id. I'm focusing on aligning the visual state of the `select` dropdown with the underlying `selectedId` state for a smoother user experience.

I'm now implementing the `text-brand-700` styling to the selected service title in the `Booking.tsx` summary.  This directly addresses the requirement for the color change. I'll make the proposed change to the `span` element within the summary section to enhance the display.

**Considering Design Principles**

I'm currently focused on implementing a "glassmorphism" aesthetic with soft shadows across the app. Right now, I'm thinking about how to integrate this, from the navigation bar to individual page layouts. I've been drilling down on the specific implementation of the glassmorphism design. I'm thinking of adding light borders, like `border-white/20`, to simulate that glass edge, alongside soft shadows for depth. I'm also considering subtly incorporating gradients in the background or containers to enhance the glass effect's visibility. I've compiled a detailed list of files, and now I'm ready to update `index.html`, the navbar, cards, forms, and panels across the app.


**Updating Footer Design**

I'm currently considering how to implement glassmorphism in the footer. Should it be subtly glassy for consistency, or remain solid for contrast? I'll start experimenting with subtle transparency and blur, but a simpler, cleaner design could work best. After all, too many elements could overwhelm the user, and a footer shouldn't steal the show.


**Applying Glass Effects**

I've got a definite plan for the app's visual overhaul. I'm focusing on those critical glassmorphism elements. I'll update the body background with a soft gradient for `index.html`. The navigation bar will use a semi-transparent white background, a slight blur, a border, and a shadow. I'll apply the style to cards across various pages, with an updated look. I'll work through the plan, from the homepage features to the admin section, ensuring a cohesive look.


**Planning CSS Implementation**

I'm currently focused on the specific HTML and JSX files that need updates to incorporate the glassmorphism effects. I've broken down the application into key components.  I will start with updating the `index.html` body to add a class. Then I will modify the `<nav>` in `Navbar.tsx` and container elements in `Home.tsx`, including sections like "Service Highlights" and "Why Choose Us." From there, it's on to `ServiceCard.tsx`, `Services.tsx`, `AMCPlans.tsx`, and finally, the `Booking.tsx` container.


**Adding Glass to Forms**

I'm now shifting my focus to implementing the glassmorphism design in various forms and containers. I'm focusing on key files like `Contact.tsx`, `Login.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, and `Admin.tsx`. I'll start by adding the `bg-white rounded-2xl` classes to these containers, ensuring a consistent look. Specifically, I'll update the contact form containers, the login box, the dashboard containers, and the stats cards and table container within the admin section. After this, I'm ready to write the code.

To implement a modern **Glassmorphism** design with soft shadows, I will apply the following changes:
